The key to solving this problem lies in understanding the divisibility rules for 12 and efficiently counting the valid combinations that satisfy these rules.

*** 

Divisibility by 12 requires a number to be divisible by both 3 and 4. Divisibility by 3 is determined by the sum of the digits being a multiple of 3, while divisibility by 4 is determined by the last two digits of the number forming a number that is divisible by 4. This means that we must ensure the sum of all digits in the number is divisible by 3 and the last two digits of the number are divisible by 4.

***

This makes the last two digits of the number $02*$, where the last digit is variable.
